methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $252.6B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
ARC Clean Technology, founded by Don Wolf, is a Series G nuclear energy company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a competitive set of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy, though the narrow peer universe constrains certain methodologies.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the company's $50M+ cumulative raise as a valuation proxy base of $252.6B and applying a 1.25x multiplier typical for Series G advancement, yielding $315.8B. A peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient comparable companies within the same stage and valuation tier; while 8 same-sector peers exist, filtering for adjacent-stage or similar-tier matches yields fewer than the three minimum comparables required for robust median analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $268.4B (low) to $363.1B (high), with a median point of $315.8B. This wide range reflects the single-method foundation of the triangulation. Key uncertainties include the reliability of total capital raised as a valuation proxy in early-stage nuclear ventures, the appropriateness of the 1.25x stage-progression multiplier for this specific sector, and the limited peer comparability within the frontier nuclear subsector. Additional data on comparable transaction multiples or recent funding rounds at similar-stage nuclear companies would materially narrow confidence intervals.
